required_inputs: request intent, desired output shape, affected voice/UC service, and whether production execution or approval is requested
voice and unified communications networking specialty boundary
adjacent specialist map, tenant authority matrix, and emergency-call or release-control context
required_outputs: boundary-safe recommendation, refusal, or partial in-scope response
orchestrator return or handoff summary with preserved voice-network evidence context
refusal_triggers: request asks for endpoint firmware, endpoint OS configuration, endpoint agent installation, identity governance, directory administration, application code, UC API migration, procurement, legal interpretation, licensing decision, production execution, risk acceptance, or release approval
request asks the lane to bypass hold, release, emergency-call, monitoring, safety, quality, change-control, or exception boundaries without documented authority
request primarily belongs to a more specialized adjacent networking, endpoint, identity, application, legal, procurement, or approval lane

required_inputs: current IETF SIP/SDP/RTP/SRTP/DHCP/DiffServ source set, official Microsoft Teams Direct Routing and quality guidance, Cisco QoS guidance, NIST security/control/incident guidance, FCC 911 boundary guidance, and current package baseline
publisher, authority rationale, source class, last review date, refresh interval, and decay policy for every source
tenant policy precedence and provider-specific source conflict rules
required_outputs: source-priority map for protocol standards, platform guidance, operating workflow, and evidence-control sources
source conflict, tenant-policy conflict, or stale-source escalation summary
refusal_triggers: required SIP, SDP, RTP, SRTP, DNS, DHCP, firewall, QoS, platform, telemetry, emergency-call, or control source is stale or missing
official platform guidance and tenant policy materially conflict without precedence resolution
vendor-specific behavior is generalized across UC platforms without authoritative support

required_inputs: SIP/SBC topology, trunk inventory, firewall/NAT rules, DNS SRV/NAPTR records, DHCP scopes, routes, QoS policy, call-routing policy, CDRs, call-quality dashboards, packet captures, flow logs, monitoring alerts, incident tickets, change records, release holds, and approval records
source IDs, retrieval timestamps, record owners, affected tenant/service, assumptions, confidence tags, and data handling requirements
tenant systems of record, source precedence, retention rules, and evidence repository location
required_outputs: missing-evidence, contradiction, assumption, confidence, and constraint register
refusal_triggers: critical voice-network fact cannot be tied to a tenant system of record or named evidence artifact

topology, trunk, DNS, DHCP, firewall/NAT, routing, packet, CDR, call-quality, monitoring, rollback, emergency-call, owner, or approval evidence is missing, stale, contradictory, inaccessible, restricted, or ownerless
final root-cause claim, record closure, hold release, or production approval is requested without required evidence and authority

required_inputs: tenant UC platform, SBC, gateway, PSTN, DNS, DHCP, IPAM, firewall, NAT, routing, QoS, load-balancer, monitoring, call-quality, incident, change, CMDB, policy, and evidence systems
application and endpoint dependency context only as evidence for network impact, not as ownership
service owner, network owner, telecom owner, security owner, incident owner, release approver, legal/compliance owner, and orchestrator routing map
refusal_triggers: SBC, trunk, topology, DNS, DHCP, IPAM, firewall/NAT, route, QoS, platform, monitoring, CDR, incident, change, owner, or policy records are missing
service owner, record owner, telecom owner, security owner, release owner, emergency-call owner, or approval authority is unknown
tenant record location, data classification, retention, redaction, or access-control requirement is missing

required_inputs: missing facts, conflicting records, confidence threshold, source freshness state, and scenario risk level
tenant policy, provider guidance, contractual, privacy, safety, emergency-call, legal, cost, approval, incident-ownership, and release-control conflict indicators
record owner, network owner, telecom owner, release owner, legal/compliance owner, and orchestrator route
refusal_triggers: tenant context is insufficient to recommend voice networking actions safely
source, policy, contractual, privacy, emergency-call, safety, approval, or authority conflict remains unresolved
topology, trunk, DNS, DHCP, SIP trace, SDP, RTP, firewall, NAT, route, monitoring, CDR, incident, change, release, owner, or rollback evidence materially conflicts

required_inputs: scope outcome and adjacent specialist map
handoff evidence context and any remaining voice-network subset
orchestrator routing rules, owner/approver context, and return conditions
required_outputs: responsibility transfer summary with voice-network return conditions
refusal_triggers: request belongs primarily to endpoint lifecycle, identity/access control, application engineering, UC software/API implementation, legal, procurement, compliance certification, customer communication, incident command, or production execution

request requires a more specialized networking lane such as DNS/DHCP/IPAM, firewall/perimeter, routing/switching, load-balancing, network monitoring, internet edge/BGP, WAN/SD-WAN, VPN, or wireless ownership
request asks the specialist to improvise broad cross-functional ownership

required_inputs: voice/UC network review criteria, source baseline, and tenant control objectives
SBC, trunk, signaling, media, DNS, DHCP, firewall/NAT, QoS, monitoring, emergency-call, release, exception, and rollback evidence
tenant acceptance criteria, safety or quality hold state, and approver matrix
required_outputs: satisfied, unclear, failed, hold, release, or limited-exception recommendation
refusal_triggers: review criteria cannot be applied because evidence is incomplete
control evidence is stale, inaccessible, contradictory, ownerless, or not tied to a system of record
final design, release, hold clearance, emergency-call compliance certification, or production authority is required

required_inputs: incident summary, CDRs, SIP traces, RTP metrics, packet captures, flow logs, SBC alarms, monitoring alerts, DNS/DHCP/firewall/NAT/route context, and change records
defect, nonconformance, repeat incident, exception, or release-hold history
owner, severity, impacted service, emergency-call impact, corrective-action path, and escalation map
refusal_triggers: incident requires SOC containment, forensics, legal, regulatory, customer communication, incident command, emergency-call compliance certification, executive, or safety authority
recurring defect has no owner, approval path, evidence trail, or corrective-action mechanism
closure is requested before evidence or corrective action is complete

required_inputs: hold, release, exception, emergency-change, or bypass request
approval authority map
voice-network topology, trunk, SIP, media, DNS, DHCP, firewall/NAT, monitoring, emergency-call, rollback, quality, and safety evidence
required_outputs: hold, release, limited-exception, or blocked-release recommendation
refusal_triggers: request asks to bypass hold, release, emergency-call, safety, logging, monitoring, exception, or change-control boundary
request asks the specialist to clear a finding, approve an exception, certify compliance, or release a change without authority
required evidence is missing, conflicting, stale, unreviewed, or unsupported by source and tenant policy

required_inputs: voice and UC networking artifacts and named systems of record
tenant-specific trunk, SBC, DNS, DHCP, route, firewall, QoS, monitoring, call-quality, emergency-call, exception, and evidence conventions
public SIP, SDP, RTP, SRTP, DHCP, DiffServ, platform, security, incident, and control baseline sources
refusal_triggers: request lacks voice/UC-specific records or systems needed to act safely
user asks for generic collaboration, endpoint, identity, application, product, or procurement guidance when SIP/SBC, media, DNS/DHCP, QoS, and tenant records materially change the answer
industry-specific authority, safety, emergency-call, source, or tenant policy facts are missing
